items.excerpt: TURKEY: Turkey's central bank kept the policy rate unchanged, maintaining a cautious stance amid heightened geopolitical uncertainty and lingering inflation risks as oil prices rise…

items.title: ECB keeps interest rates on hold, avoids rattling markets
items.excerpt: EUROPE: The ECB has kept interest rates on hold. Yet, with energy prices soaring, and assuming the June hike was not only an insurance move, a hike in September looks almost like a done…

items.title: Rates Spark: ECB ready to hike, just not today
items.excerpt: The ECB should keep the policy rate at 2.25%, but we do see a September hike as likely, especially as oil prices are moving higher again. One could argue for a front-loaded hike today, but over previous years the ECB has always fully telegraphed any policy moves in advance. And with no hikes priced in by markets, deviating from this strategy seems…

items.title: FX Daily: Can the ECB break the euro’s low-volatility torpor?
items.excerpt: The ECB should keep rates on hold today, but we think policymakers will focus on preserving the hawkish market pricing. We suspect the now familiar post-meeting media leak will signal a September rate hike. That may keep EUR/USD supported from a rate perspective, delaying a break lower. In Australia, blowout jobs numbers are helping the bullish AUD…

items.author: Saxo Bank

items.title: Sterling’s rally is not built to last
items.excerpt: Sterling’s rally has been driven more by positioning, carry and potentially some M A flows than by a lasting improvement in UK fundamentals. With UK short-dated rates likely to drift lower and fiscal risks set to return ahead of the autumn, we expect sterling to hand back recent gains. Our central view remains for EUR/GBP to rise towards 0.88 by…

items.title: Euro futures rise as ECB and Fed rate decisions approach.
items.excerpt: Bob Iaccino breaks down the latest price action in Euro futures as the September contract bounces following four consecutive lower closes. With the ECB meeting approaching, markets are pricing high expectations for unchanged rates following cooled Eurozone inflation data. Meanwhile, the upcoming Federal Reserve meeting keeps the U.S. and European interest rate gap intact, with geopolitical conflict in the Middle East remaining a key variable for central bank outlooks.
items.author: CME Group

items.title: Stocks pull back while crude surges on geopolitical deadlock.
items.excerpt: Bob Iaccino breaks down the morning market action as U.S. stock futures pull back and crude oil rises amid deadlocked U.S.-Iran peace negotiations. Meanwhile, gold gains ground, Treasury yields edge higher, and traders await key earnings from Alphabet and Tesla alongside the upcoming ECB rate decision.

items.title: Investment lifts Polish second-quarter growth despite consumption slowdown
items.excerpt: CENTRAL AND EASTERN EUROPE: Despite stronger-than-expected growth in June, second-quarter retail sales growth was slower than in 1Q26, pointing to a moderation in private consumption growth. Nevertheless, robust fixed investment growth more than offset weaker consumption spending, lifting GDP growth to around 3.8% YoY in the second quarter from 3.5% YoY in the…

items.title: LVMH through results season: what mini options change for a smaller portfolio
items.excerpt: A single LVMH option contract commits nearly EUR 49,000. The mini contract commits a tenth of that, which changes who can act on a view before the 27 July results. One standard option contract on LVMH covers about EUR 48,700 of stock. A mini contract covers a tenth of that, and for many investors the difference decides whether these tools are usable at all. LVMH closed at EUR 486.85 on Monday 20 July 2026 (Source: Bloomberg and SaxoTrader, 21 July 2026). For many European investors it is a long-term holding whose case has become more of a debate. First-quarter revenue was EUR 19.1 billion with organic growth of 1%, below consensus, held back by a currency drag the company put at 7% of reported revenue. Fashion and Leather Goods fell 2% organically while Watches and Jewellery grew 7%…

items.author: Saxo Bank

items.title: Euro futures fell for a 4th straight session near 1.14.
items.excerpt: Euro futures moved lower, closing near 1.14 to mark a fourth consecutive daily decline. Strength in the U.S. Dollar, fueled by uncertainty around Middle East tensions, oil prices, and Federal Reserve policy, continued to weigh on the currency. Market focus turns to the European Central Bank meeting later this week, where no policy changes are expected, though traders remain attentive to future rate path expectations. Meanwhile, CVOL index data shows declining euro volatility, and CFTC positioning data reveals that speculators expanded their net short euro positions for only the second time since March 2025.
items.author: CME Group

items.title: Euro futures fell for a third session as the dollar firmed.
items.excerpt: Dan Deming of KKM Financial breaks down the latest price action in the FX markets, focusing on the recent slide in Euro futures. The currency fell for a third consecutive session, trading back into the middle of its one-month range near 1.144. Deming discusses the primary catalysts behind the move, highlighting how escalated tensions in the Middle East are providing short-term support to the U.S. dollar. Additionally, he analyzes the impact of the latest ECB economic report, which has added further pressure to the euro. While British Pound futures and Japanese Yen futures also showed weakness, Australian Dollar futures managed to find support as an outlier.
items.author: CME Group

items.title: Geopolitical risks drive oil as stocks bounce.
items.excerpt: Bob Iaccino breaks down the morning's market action, noting that stocks are bouncing despite an uptick in U.S. Treasury yields. He covers the latest movements in silver, copper, and major currencies against a stronger U.S. dollar. Highlighting renewed geopolitical tensions, including U.S.-Iran strikes and shipping concerns, Iaccino explains the recent spike and partial reversal in crude oil prices. He also analyzes the diverging paths of gold and Bitcoin, outlining the different forces driving these markets. Finally, Iaccino previews upcoming earnings reports from major companies and regional banks, alongside key global economic data releases.

items.author: Saxo Bank

items.title: Euro futures slipped as lower inflation supports policy pause.
items.excerpt: September Euro futures experienced minor daily losses but managed a weekly gain of 0.14%, continuing to carve out a bottom over the past four weeks. The currency found support after Eurostat reported that June Eurozone inflation cooled significantly to 2.8%, down from 3.2% in May and coming in below market expectations. This softer macroeconomic reading gives the European Central Bank enhanced flexibility to hold interest rates steady at 2.25% during its upcoming meeting. However, war-driven energy risks remain a primary concern for policy makers, contrasting with weaker U.S. inflation data that has cooled the Federal Reserve's tightening narrative.
items.author: CME Group

items.title: Tech weakness and ECB rate decision set the tone.
items.excerpt: The upcoming week tests global markets as the recent 3% drop in the Nasdaq-100 risks pulling broader indices lower. With the Dow and Russell already extending their consecutive weekly declines, investors are closely watching to see if capital rotates back into technology or if the selling pressure persists. Across the Atlantic, the ECB's rate decision and Christine Lagarde's press conference take center stage. Diverging monetary policy narratives between the ECB and the Fed could spark significant volatility across European and U.S. equities, gold, and the U.S. dollar.
items.author: CME Group

items.title: Commodities weekly: war, weather and backwardation drive returns
items.excerpt: Key points: The Bloomberg Commodity Total Return Index has gained 5.3% so far in July and 20.5% year to date, but the advance has been heavily concentrated in energy and agriculture. Renewed Middle East hostilities, disrupted trade flows and strongly backwardated energy curves have delivered exceptional returns across crude oil and refined products. Agriculture may face further volatility as El Ni o strengthens, while Black Sea export risks and the biofuel link continue to support selected grain and oilseed markets. Longer-term trend signals remain broadly constructive, but short-term momentum has become increasingly fragmented, particularly across crude oil and precious metals. As we move beyond the midpoint of July and into the holiday season, when many traders, investors and analysts…
items.author: Ole Hansen

items.title: Hedging a portfolio near the highs: four ways to buy insurance while it is cheap
items.excerpt: An overnight tech-led selloff has nudged volatility up from subdued levels - a reminder, not yet a regime change - and index protection is still historically cheap. This article prices four ways to hedge a broad equity book off the SPX chain (a 16 July snapshot) - a protective put, a collar, a put-spread collar and a deep tail overlay - so the cost-versus-protection trade-off is visible in dollars. A calm tape is when you build the shelter; an overnight wobble is the reminder and even with volatility ticking up, index insurance is still not expensive. This article was priced on 16 July 2026, when the S P 500 sat around 7,560 roughly 0.7% below its 2 June closing high of 7,620.90 and the VIX was near 16, toward the lower end of its multi-year range (Source: exchange data, Cboe). Overnight…

items.title: British Pound futures retreated as U.S. dollar rebounded.
items.excerpt: British Pound futures moved lower as the U.S. dollar recovered ground following recent weakness. After reaching high levels earlier in the week driven by inflation data and Fed testimony, the pound reversed course to trade near 1.3465. CME Group's CVOL index indicated that while daily volatility eased alongside falling prices, weekly volatility metrics remain elevated. Key economic indicators, including import and export prices, University of Michigan consumer sentiment, and housing data, are scheduled for release ahead of the weekend.
items.author: CME Group
